source.description: Six-panel marker process contact sheet regenerated for an owner-requested correction, then cropped into public step frames and a finished image.
source.notes: Corrected after review because the original July 4 grill looked like a lid was sitting behind the grate and flames. The replacement keeps the grill open with no lid behind the fire.

process_strategy: A six-panel process builds an open kettle grill bowl first, then adds legs, wheels, rim, grate, flames, smoke curls, marker fills, and final outline cleanup without placing a lid behind the fire.

final_elements.name: open round grill bowl
final_elements.description: A red open kettle grill bowl with a clear top rim and no lid sitting behind the flames.

final_elements.name: short legs and wheels
final_elements.description: Three short supports with two small wheels under the grill.

final_elements.name: top rim and side handles
final_elements.description: A thick rim around the open bowl plus small side handles attached to the bowl.

final_elements.name: grate and flame marks
final_elements.description: A simple grate sitting across the open top with small flame shapes rising above it.

final_elements.name: smoke curls
final_elements.description: A few curved smoke lines rising from the grill.

final_elements.name: red and charcoal marker fills
final_elements.description: Bold red marker on the grill shell, dark charcoal legs and grate, orange flames, and gray smoke.

final_elements.name: white shine gaps
final_elements.description: Small uncolored or gel-pen shine gaps on the existing grill shell.

frames.visible_job: Places the open round grill bowl guide with a clear top opening.
Adds short supports and small wheels under the existing grill body.
Adds a thick top rim and small side handles to the existing open bowl.
Adds the grate, flame shapes, and smoke curls to existing grill parts.
Adds red, charcoal, orange, gray, and shine accents only to shapes already present.

final_step.visible_job: Only thickens the existing black outlines, deepens the marker fills, sharpens shine gaps, and strengthens the smoke, flame, and sticker shadow accents.

rejection_checks: No new leg, wheel, rim, side handle, grate, smoke curl, flame, or color area should first appear in the final image.
The grill must keep the same open bowl shape, angle, and scale in every frame.
No domed lid should sit behind the grate or flames.
The finish must look like a handmade marker doodle, not a clean vector icon or polished poster.
